title: rosa canina var. pseudostylosa
intro: Rosa canina var. pseudostylosa, also known as the dwarf dog rose or the shrubby dog rose, is a variety of wild rose that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant's medicinal properties include its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and carotenoids, which are known to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. pseudostylosa include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Rosa canina var. pseudostylosa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
